description: Persist a default-disabled login CAPTCHA setting in the database upgrade flow.
steps: Add a database migration for one persisted CAPTCHA settings record with enabled set to false by default.
Store provider selection and provider parameters in a shape that can support Cloudflare Turnstile, hCaptcha, Google reCAPTCHA, Tencent Cloud CAPTCHA, Alibaba Cloud CAPTCHA 2.0, and GeeTest CAPTCHA.
Register the migration after the current latest migration without changing existing migration order.
Call the admin database status or upgrade API in tests and verify the required database version includes the new CAPTCHA migration.
Verify the upgraded database reads CAPTCHA as disabled when no admin has configured it.

description: Expose the login page CAPTCHA challenge state without requiring an admin session.
steps: Add a public read-only API response for the admin login page that reports whether CAPTCHA is enabled.
Return only the selected provider and browser-safe public parameters when CAPTCHA is enabled.
Ensure stored secret values are never present in the public response body.
Request the API with the default database state and verify it returns enabled false.
Request the API after a seeded enabled setting and verify it returns only the expected public provider fields.

description: Let signed-in admins read CAPTCHA settings without exposing stored secrets.
steps: Add an authenticated admin API that returns the current CAPTCHA enabled flag, provider, and saved public parameters.
Represent saved secret parameters with redacted markers or omit them from the response.
Request the API without a valid admin session and verify it returns 401.
Request the API with a valid admin session and verify the default response is disabled.
Seed an enabled provider setting and verify the admin read response does not expose raw secret values.

description: Let signed-in admins save a disabled CAPTCHA configuration safely.
steps: Add an authenticated admin update API that accepts enabled false without provider-specific parameters.
Persist the disabled state while preserving unrelated database data and existing admin sessions.
Send an authenticated update request with enabled false and verify the response reports CAPTCHA disabled.
Read the settings again through the admin API and verify the disabled state survives a second request.
Read the public login challenge API after disabling and verify it reports enabled false.

description: Validate and persist Turnstile, hCaptcha, and reCAPTCHA settings.
steps: Accept Cloudflare Turnstile, hCaptcha, and Google reCAPTCHA as explicit provider values in the admin update API.
Require the browser public key and server secret key fields when one of these providers is enabled.
Send a valid update request for each provider and verify the saved provider can be read back with secrets redacted.
Send an enabled update request with a missing required key and verify the API returns 400.
After the rejected request, read settings again and verify the previous persisted setting was not changed.

description: Validate and persist Tencent Cloud, Alibaba Cloud 2.0, and GeeTest CAPTCHA settings.
steps: Accept Tencent Cloud CAPTCHA, Alibaba Cloud CAPTCHA 2.0, and GeeTest CAPTCHA as explicit provider values in the admin update API.
Require the provider-specific browser public parameters and server secret credentials when one of these providers is enabled.
Send a valid update request for Tencent Cloud CAPTCHA and verify the saved provider can be read back with secrets redacted.
Send a valid update request for Alibaba Cloud CAPTCHA 2.0 and GeeTest CAPTCHA and verify each saved provider can be read back with secrets redacted.
Send an unsupported provider value and verify the API returns 400 without changing the previous setting.

description: Verify Turnstile, hCaptcha, and reCAPTCHA tokens before admin password authentication.
steps: Add a provider verification path for Cloudflare Turnstile, hCaptcha, and Google reCAPTCHA using the configured server secret key.
Update the admin login API request contract to accept a CAPTCHA response token when CAPTCHA is enabled.
Post a login request with CAPTCHA enabled and no token and verify it fails before password authentication.
Mock each provider verification response and verify a successful CAPTCHA result allows the existing password login flow to continue.
Mock a failed verification response and verify the login API rejects the request without creating a session cookie.

description: Verify Tencent Cloud, Alibaba Cloud 2.0, and GeeTest CAPTCHA payloads before admin password authentication.
steps: Add provider verification paths for Tencent Cloud CAPTCHA, Alibaba Cloud CAPTCHA 2.0, and GeeTest CAPTCHA using the saved server credentials.
Update the admin login API request contract to accept the provider-specific CAPTCHA payload fields required by these providers.
Post a login request with each provider enabled and a missing CAPTCHA payload and verify it fails before password authentication.
Mock successful provider verification responses and verify the existing password login flow still returns the admin session response.
Mock failed provider verification responses and verify the login API rejects the request without creating a session cookie.

description: Show an admin CAPTCHA settings page for choosing a provider and editing parameters.
steps: Add an admin navigation entry and page route for CAPTCHA settings without changing existing admin page routes.
Render an enable switch, provider selector, and provider-specific parameter inputs for all six supported providers.
Load the current settings from the admin read API and save changes through the admin update API.
Open the settings page in a browser and verify the default disabled state is shown.
Switch between providers in the browser and verify only the selected provider parameter fields are visible.

description: Connect the admin login page to the configured CAPTCHA challenge flow.
steps: Load the public CAPTCHA challenge API when the admin login screen is shown in the browser.
Verify the default disabled state keeps the login form visually unchanged and submits without CAPTCHA fields.
Seed an enabled provider setting and verify the login page renders a provider-specific challenge container or script hook before submit.
Submit a browser login attempt with a mocked CAPTCHA response and verify the login request includes the expected CAPTCHA payload.
Submit a browser login attempt without the CAPTCHA response while enabled and verify the UI shows the backend validation error.
